258.title: Roasted Carrot and Lentil Salad
ing: carrots, peeled and chopped: the star of the show! roasting brings out their natural sweetness and creates tender, slightly caramelized edges. use regular orange carrots, or mix in rainbow carrots for extra visual appeal. chop them into roughly ½-inch thick rounds or half-moons for even cooking
olive oil, divided: used for roasting the carrots and forming the base of the vinaigrette. choose a good quality extra virgin olive oil for the best flavour
cumin seeds: adds a warm, earthy, slightly smoky flavour that pairs beautifully with carrots and lentils. toasting whole seeds briefly before grinding enhances their aroma
teaspoon smoked paprika: contributes a subtle smokiness and beautiful colour to the roasted carrots
salt and freshly ground black pepper: essential for seasoning the carrots and the final salad. adjust to your personal taste

brown or green lentils, rinsed: these varieties hold their shape well after cooking, providing a satisfying texture. avoid red lentils, which tend to become mushy. rinsing removes any debris and surface starch
water or vegetable broth: for cooking the lentils. using broth adds an extra layer of flavour, but water works perfectly fine
bay leaf: infuses the lentils with a subtle herbal aroma as they cook
cup extra virgin olive oil: forms the base of our vibrant vinaigrette
apple cider vinegar: provides the essential acidity and tang to balance the sweetness of the carrots and the earthiness of the lentils. lemon juice offers a brighter citrus note
dijon mustard: helps emulsify the vinaigrette and adds a pungent kick
maple syrup: adds a touch of sweetness to balance the vinegar’s tang and complement the roasted carrots. adjust the amount based on your preference
garlic, minced: adds a pungent depth to the dressing. use fresh garlic for the best flavour
cup chopped fresh parsley: brings freshness, colour, and a clean, herbaceous flavour that brightens the entire dish
cup chopped fresh mint: adds a lovely cool, refreshing counterpoint to the warm spices
cup toasted pumpkin seeds or almond slivers: provides essential crunch and nutty flavour. toasting enhances their taste and texture
feta cheese, crumbled: adds a salty, tangy creaminess that contrasts beautifully with the other ingredients
baby spinach or arugula: to serve the lentil and carrot mixture over a bed of greens for a more traditional salad feel. arugula adds a peppery bite, while spinach is milder

bell peppers: use whatever colors you have on hand. if you prefer a milder taste, go with red or yellow peppers as they are sweeter
jalapeno: for less heat, omit the jalapeno or replace it with a small amount of chopped green bell pepper. if you like it spicier, leave in the seeds or try a serrano pepper
red onion: can be substituted with white or sweet onions
cilantro: if you’re not a fan, fresh parsley or a touch of mint can add a different but still refreshing note
garlic and ginger powder: these spices add depth. you can also use freshly grated garlic and ginger if preferred

454.title: Overnight Oats with Berries
ing: rolled oats: the star of the show! rolled oats, also known as old-fashioned oats, are the best choice for overnight oats. they absorb liquid beautifully, resulting in a creamy and satisfying texture without becoming mushy. avoid using instant oats, as they can become too soft, and steel-cut oats, which require longer soaking and might remain too chewy for some preferences in overnight oats

milk: the liquid base for your oats. you can use any type of milk you prefer. dairy milk, such as cow’s milk, adds richness and creaminess. for a vegan or dairy-free option, almond milk, soy milk, oat milk, coconut milk, or cashew milk work wonderfully. each non-dairy milk will impart a slightly different flavor profile to your oats, allowing for further customization

chia seeds: these tiny seeds are nutritional powerhouses and essential for achieving the right consistency in overnight oats. chia seeds are highly absorbent and create a pudding-like texture as they soak up liquid. they also add a boost of fiber, omega-3 fatty acids, and protein

yogurt: adding yogurt takes the creaminess factor up a notch and introduces beneficial probiotics. plain greek yogurt is a popular choice for extra protein and tang, but regular yogurt or dairy-free yogurt alternatives like coconut yogurt or almond milk yogurt also work well. if you prefer a lighter texture or are dairy-free, you can omit the yogurt

berries: the burst of fruity sweetness and vibrant color! you can use a mix of your favorite berries. fresh berries like blueberries, raspberries, strawberries, and blackberries are delicious and provide antioxidants. frozen berries are equally nutritious and often more budget-friendly, especially out of season. they also help keep your oats cold and can be added directly to the jar without thawing

sweetener: to enhance the sweetness, you can add a touch of your preferred sweetener. maple syrup, honey, agave nectar, or even a few drops of stevia or monk fruit sweetener are great options. adjust the amount to your taste, or omit it entirely if you prefer a less sweet breakfast, especially if your berries are already quite sweet

vanilla extract: a splash of vanilla extract enhances the overall flavor profile, adding warmth and depth. it complements the oats and berries beautifully, creating a more rounded and delicious breakfast. a little goes a long way!

salt: just a tiny pinch of salt can actually enhance the sweetness and balance the flavors in your overnight oats. salt helps to bring out the natural flavors of the other ingredients, making them taste even better

i used an italian spice blend that you can find at any grocery store. mine is a combo of basil, oregano, thyme, rosemary, savory, fennel, sage and marjoram. some versions have red pepper or salt, so i would avoid those as it could make the soup really spicy
